title: leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. cuneata
intro: Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. cuneata, also known as the cushioned white everlasting, is a low-growing, alpine perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Leucanthemopsis alpina subsp. cuneata include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
